text: can you add annotation on what each data set (gray and yellow are used to define crime, in some other cities this value
producerNote: Done at the layer the watcher owns: verified the engine-wide SeamCallout ('WHY THE JUMP?', FullTrend.tsx L189-225) names each era — gray = FBI classic index crimes, city color = broader incident-based system — and states the jump is the classification ruler changing, not the city. DC re-render is tracked by an existing confidence.json blocker (feedback 2026-07-18); the render itself belongs to the driver/producer, not the watcher.

text: the value jumps in some cities when data set source changes, and it would be to have a few word on how they can be different and does crime clasification has been updated?
producerNote: Answered at DC's config layer: added a verified DC-specific seamExplain to the 'WHY THE JUMP?' card — through 2007 = DC's yearly UCR figures reported to the FBI (classic index crimes); from 2008 = MPD's own incident feed (different source/taxonomy, logged case by case, still major Part-I offenses), so the jump is the measuring system changing, not a re-classification of crime. This corrects the generic card's 'broader' overstatement for DC (MPD publishes only major offenses). Config-only change; re-render is tracked by the confidence.json blocker — GPU belongs to the producer.

text: RE-RENDER ITEM (engine zero-count 'safest' fix): the shipped washington-dc.mp4 reveal/quiz answers 'Observatory Circle: 0' — a no-data polygon (US Naval Observatory; MPD publishes no incidents there), not a safe neighborhood. The engine now excludes zero-record polygons from the safest ranking (derive.ts hoodRanking allTotal>0 filter + disclosed caveat), so the next re-render answers 'Rock Creek Park: 15' and the quiz options change accordingly. No data or config change needed for DC — engine-only. Queue this with the already-pending DC re-render (seamExplain blocker); re-renders are HELD until the citywide-trend audit reports.
producerNote: Root cause: engine, not DC — derive.ts hoodRanking ranked no-data window polygons as 'safest'; fixed engine-wide via merged PR #4 (allTotal>0 filter + hoodNoDataCount caveat). Independently re-verified DC on the render's exact 60-month window slice (2021-07..2026-06): pre-fix answer = Observatory Circle[all=0] (no-data US Naval Observatory), post-fix = Rock Creek Park[gA=15] — exactly as noted. The DC entry's existing re-render blocker now carries this fix (confidence.json), so the held re-render picks it up with seamExplain; watcher never renders. Window-aware sweep of all 20 cities confirms only ATL/MKE/DC affected — DC is the only SHIPPED artifact; MKE/ATL are unrendered so their first render is auto-correct.

Owner APPROVE recorded as verify-light evidence in confidence.json history (verify is a manual light — flips on this Approve). Root cause of a stray open blocker found + fixed: the 00:43 re-render satisfied BOTH the zero-count AND the seamExplain re-render blockers, but the producer's hand-maintained clear removed only the zero-count — leaving the seamExplain re-render blocker stale (blocker-drift category). Independently verified the seam fix is LIVE (extracted still @96s of the current mp4: the 'WHY THE JUMP?' card renders the DC-specific seamExplain copy — FBI UCR gray 1985-2007 vs MPD gold 2008+, dashed 2008 seam) and cleared the stale blocker. Left untouched (not gated on this approve): full watch-through with audio (separate manual gate, boston precedent) + society=0 counter (genuine honesty item). Sweep: DC is the only open owner APPROVE this run; boston reconciled in a prior run.

text: the thumbnail never landed as an option when I uploaded private first and then i was only able to upload the thumbnail after and then I flipped to to publish from private, so fix the thumbnail flow so it actually is included with the publish buttons
producerNote: Root cause (dashboard engine, not DC-specific): the custom-thumbnail step (YouTube thumbnails.set) fired exactly ONCE, immediately after videos.insert, best-effort + silent — but a freshly-uploaded video is still PROCESSING, so the call hits the transient 'video not found / still processing' race and fails; the video landed (private) with no custom thumbnail and the only recovery was a manual YouTube-Studio upload + flip-to-public (exactly your experience). There was also no studio-side way to re-apply the thumbnail after upload. FIX at the shared publish engine (server.mjs — covers all 20 cities identically, so this IS the category sweep; server.mjs is the sole thumbnails.set path in the tree): (1) applyThumbnail() retries the transient race with backoff (403 unverified / 400 bad-image stay permanent, no pointless retries) instead of one silent shot; (2) new guarded POST /api/publish/<slug>/setthumb + pushThumbnail() re-applies the committed thumbnail.jpg to an already-uploaded video (409 verified on an unpublished city — never calls YouTube without a videoId); (3) the thumbnail action now lives WITH the publish buttons in all three surfaces (index.html): the publish result shows a '⤴ Push thumbnail' retry when it didn't land, the already-published modal and the published-video detail page both carry '⤴ Push your/this thumbnail to YouTube' — no YouTube-Studio trip. Service restarted + verified via curl (catalog/publishPreview shapes intact, setthumb guard 409, served page carries all wiring). No upload triggered by me. Sweep breadth: 1 engine path = all 20 cities; both live videos (boston/DC) render the new button.

Owner re-APPROVE recorded as verify-light evidence in confidence.json history. Root cause this checkpoint catches: the d829e57 countTerm re-render changed on-screen wording (GROUP A -> REPORTED) AFTER the 15:28 approve but its producer note omitted a verify-restale flag (label-only-re-render drift). Confirmed the SHIPPED bytes match the approve before signing: mp4 sha256 == render.lock (583ccd304a21 / commit d829e57), and 3 stills off that exact encode show REPORTED with zero 'GROUP A' (t=200), the Rock Creek Park 'fewest reported' reveal not Observatory Circle:0 (t=306), and the honest 2008 measure-change seam (t=90). No stale re-render blocker to strip (all cleared prior runs); retained watch-through (manual gate) + society=0 (genuine honesty item). Score held 91 (no double-inflation on a re-approved, already-counted honest cut). Grand-rapids owner APPROVE (22:54) is a separate cut needing its own still-verify + has an in-flight render.lock edit — left for next run, not batched.

text: <CRITIC/videos> DC's composed thumbnail.jpg (12:15) predates the 16:19 re-render that was then published at 16:44 — verify the composed frame content against the CURRENT render's frames (candidates now auto-refresh via the new staleness rule in ensureThumbs) and recompose + thumbnails.set on the live video if anything visible changed (esp. the reveal: Rock Creek Park fix). Why: a published thumbnail must be a frame of the published cut.
